texts.label: text
texts.orig: Lambok and his elder sister Phiban live in Shillong. One day they came home from school. Their parents were away at work and Lambok complained that he was feeling feverish. Phiban touched his forehead and felt that he might be having a fever. To confirm this, she took out the thermometer kept in the almirah. She washed its tip with soap and water, and measured Lambok's temperature. To her relief, she found that his temperature was normal. She washed the thermometer tip again, dried it and put it back. She then asked Lambok to change his school uniform, eat his lunch, and rest for some time.
texts.text: Lambok and his elder sister Phiban live in Shillong. One day they came home from school. Their parents were away at work and Lambok complained that he was feeling feverish. Phiban touched his forehead and felt that he might be having a fever. To confirm this, she took out the thermometer kept in the almirah. She washed its tip with soap and water, and measured Lambok's temperature. To her relief, she found that his temperature was normal. She washed the thermometer tip again, dried it and put it back. She then asked Lambok to change his school uniform, eat his lunch, and rest for some time.

texts.orig: We cannot always rely upon our sense of touch to decide correctly whether a body is hot or cold.
texts.text: We cannot always rely upon our sense of touch to decide correctly whether a body is hot or cold.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 7.2 Temperature
texts.text: 7.2 Temperature

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A reliable measure of hotness (or coldness) of a body is its temperature. A hotter body has a higher temperature than a colder body. The difference in temperature between the two bodies tells us how hot a body is in comparison to another body. A device that measures temperature is called a thermometer.
texts.text: A reliable measure of hotness (or coldness) of a body is its temperature. A hotter body has a higher temperature than a colder body. The difference in temperature between the two bodies tells us how hot a body is in comparison to another body. A device that measures temperature is called a thermometer.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: There are two kinds of thermometers that you are likely to come across—clinical thermometers and laboratory thermometers. Clinical thermometers are used to measure human body temperatures whereas laboratory thermometers are used for many other purposes. Let us now learn more about thermometers and how to use them to measure temperature.
texts.text: There are two kinds of thermometers that you are likely to come across—clinical thermometers and laboratory thermometers. Clinical thermometers are used to measure human body temperatures whereas laboratory thermometers are used for many other purposes. Let us now learn more about thermometers and how to use them to measure temperature.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The temperature of every person may not be 37.0 °C. What we call normal temperature is the average body temperature of a large number of healthy people. A_ perfectly healthy person may, therefore, have a normal temperature slightly different from 37.0 °C. The body temperature is influenced by several factors, such as age, time of the day and activity level.
texts.text: The temperature of every person may not be 37.0 °C. What we call normal temperature is the average body temperature of a large number of healthy people. A_ perfectly healthy person may, therefore, have a normal temperature slightly different from 37.0 °C. The body temperature is influenced by several factors, such as age, time of the day and activity level.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: You may try measuring your own temperature at different times of the day and on different days. Record the thermometer readings in your notebook. After a month, analyse your temperature record and see if there are any variations. If yes, try to think what might be the reasons for that.
texts.text: You may try measuring your own temperature at different times of the day and on different days. Record the thermometer readings in your notebook. After a month, analyse your temperature record and see if there are any variations. If yes, try to think what might be the reasons for that.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The temperature of human beings does not normally go below 35 °C or above 42 °C.
texts.text: The temperature of human beings does not normally go below 35 °C or above 42 °C.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: For measuring the body temperature of small children or old people, the digital thermometer can also be placed in the armpit. The temperature measured this way is about 0.5 °C to 1 °C lower than the actual body temperature.
texts.text: For measuring the body temperature of small children or old people, the digital thermometer can also be placed in the armpit. The temperature measured this way is about 0.5 °C to 1 °C lower than the actual body temperature.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: There is another scale of temperature known as Fahrenheit scale. On this scale, the unit oftemperature is degree Fahrenheit and is denoted by °F. A temperature measured as 37.0 °C on Celsius scale is equivalent to 98.6 °F on Fahrenheit scale. The Fahrenheit scale is not used in most scientific studies anymore. In scientific work, there is another scale of temperature known as Kelvin scale. On this scale, the unit for temperature is kelvin and is denoted by K. The SI unit of temperature is kelvin.
texts.text: There is another scale of temperature known as Fahrenheit scale. On this scale, the unit oftemperature is degree Fahrenheit and is denoted by °F. A temperature measured as 37.0 °C on Celsius scale is equivalent to 98.6 °F on Fahrenheit scale. The Fahrenheit scale is not used in most scientific studies anymore. In scientific work, there is another scale of temperature known as Kelvin scale. On this scale, the unit for temperature is kelvin and is denoted by K. The SI unit of temperature is kelvin.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: All three temperature scales, Celsius, Fahrenheit and Kelvin are named in honour of the scientists who developed these scales.
texts.text: All three temperature scales, Celsius, Fahrenheit and Kelvin are named in honour of the scientists who developed these scales.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: We can easily convert the temperature from Celsius scale to Kelvin scale by using:
texts.text: We can easily convert the temperature from Celsius scale to Kelvin scale by using:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Temperature in Kelvin scale = Temperature in Celsius scale + 273.15
texts.text: Temperature in Kelvin scale -Temperature in Celsius scale + 273.15

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The names of temperature scales—Celsius scale, Fahrenheit scale and Kelvin scale—start with a capital letter. For the units for temperature, degree Celsius and degree Fahrenheit, the word degree starts with a lower-case letter while Celsius and Fahrenheit start with a capital letter. The unit kelvin starts with a lower-case letter. The symbols of all units (°C, °F, K) are capital letters. Note that degree sign (°) is not written with K. A full stop is not written after the symbol, except at the end of a sentence. While writing the temperature, a space is left between the number and the unit. For temperatures more than one degree, use the plural of 'degree', that is, 'degrees', while writing the full form of the unit.

A full Stop is not written after the symbol, except at the end of a sentence. While writing the temperature, a space is left between the number and the unit. For temperatures more than one degree, use the plural of 'degree', that is, 'degrees', while writing the full form of the unit.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 7.3.2 Laboratory thermometer
texts.text: 7.3.2 Laboratory thermometer

texts.label: text
texts.orig: There are many types of laboratory thermometers but the one that might be available in your school laboratory may look like the one shown in Fig. 7.3a. It consists of a long, narrow, uniform glass tube which is sealed. At one end of the tube is a bulb which contains a liquid. Outside the bulb, in the tube, a narrow column of liquid can be seen. There is a Celsius scale marked along the tube. The liquid column rises or falls with change in temperature. The mark of the Celsius scale with which the top level of the liquid column coincides is the temperature reading.
texts.text: There are many types of laboratory thermometers but the one that might be available in your school laboratory may look like the one shown in Fig. 7.3a. It consists of a long, narrow, uniform glass tube which is sealed. At one end of the tube is a bulb which contains a liquid. Outside the bulb, in the tube, a narrow column of liquid can be seen. There is a Celsius scale marked along the tube. The liquid column rises or falls with change in temperature. The mark of the Celsius scale with which the top level of the liquid column coincides is the temperature reading.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Fig. 7.3b shows a closeup of a part of the thermometer shown in Fig. 7.3a. Can you now find the smallest value that this thermometer can read?
texts.text: Fig. 7.3b shows a closeup of a part of the thermometer shown in Fig. 7.3a. Can you now find the smallest value that this thermometer can read?

texts.label: text
texts.orig: For the thermometer shown in Fig. 7.3b, the temperature difference indicated between 0 °C and 10 °C or between 10 °C and 20 °C is 10 °C. And the number of divisions between these marks are 10 divisions. So, one small division can read 10/10 = 1 °C.
texts.text: For the thermometer shown in Fig. 7.3b, the temperature difference indicated between 0 °C and 10 °C or between 10 °C and 20 °C is 10 °C. And the number of divisions between these marks are 10 divisions. So, one small division can read 10/10 = 1 °C.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: That is, the smallest value that this thermometer can read is 1 °C.
texts.text: That is, the smallest value that this thermometer can read is 1 °C.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Your school laboratory may have thermometers for which the range and the value of the smallest division may be different. It is, therefore, always necessary to look carefully at the thermometer you are about to use.

texts.orig: Does the maximum and minimum temperature stay at the same level during these days?
texts.text: Does the maximum and minimum temperature stay at the same level during these days?

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Because weather depends on several factors, these temperatures usually vary every day. Generally, as we approach the summer season, the temperature rises and during the winter season it falls.
texts.text: Because weather depends on several factors, these temperatures usually vary every day. Generally, as we approach the summer season, the temperature rises and during the winter season it falls.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Anna Mani (1918-2001) was an Indian scientist, also known as the 'Weather Woman of India'. She invented and built a large number of weather measurement instruments. This reduced the reliance of India on other nations for such instruments. She also explored the possibilities of using wind and solar energy in India. Her work helped India to become one of the global leaders in renewable energy.
texts.text: Anna Mani (1918-2001) was an Indian scientist, also known as the 'Weather Woman of India'. She invented and built a large number of weather measurement instruments. This reduced the reliance of India on other nations for such instruments. She also explored the possibilities of using wind and solar energy in India. Her work helped India to become one of the global leaders in renewable energy.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The temperature of a body tells us how hot or cold it is.
texts.text: The temperature of a body tells us how hot or cold it is.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The three most-used scales of temperature are: (a) the Celsius scale, (b) the Fahrenheit scale, and (c) the Kelvin scale. The units of temperature in these scales are (a) degree Celsius, denoted by °C, (b) degree Fahrenheit, denoted by °F, and (c) kelvin, denoted by K.
texts.text: The three most-used scales of temperature are: (a) the Celsius scale, (b) the Fahrenheit scale, and (c) the Kelvin scale. The units of temperature in these scales are (a) degree Celsius, denoted by °C, (b) degree Fahrenheit, denoted by °F, and (c) kelvin, denoted by K.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The SI unit for temperature is kelvin.
texts.text: The SI unit for temperature is kelvin.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Aclinical thermometer is used for measuring body temperature.
texts.text: Aclinical thermometer is used for measuring body temperature.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Normal temperature of a healthy human adult is taken to be 37.0 °C or 98.6 °F.
texts.text: Normal temperature of a healthy human adult is taken to be 37.0 °C or 98.6 °F.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Laboratory thermometers typically have a temperature range from -10 °C to 110 °C.
texts.text: Laboratory thermometers typically have a temperature range from -10 °C to 110 °C.

texts.orig: Gather information from the Internet and find out how the body temperature of animals, such as a cat, dog, horse, camel, cow and buffalo, is measured. If there is any veterinary hospital in your vicinity, you may visit to see the body temperature of animals being measured.

texts.orig: Find out which places in India are usually regarded to be the coldest and hottest. Also, find out the minimum and the maximum temperatures recorded for these places.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Various planets in our Solar System are at different distances from the Sun. Search the Internet and make a table with the planets, their distances from the Sun (in increasing order) and their temperatures written. Does the average temperature of planets decrease as their distance from the Sun increases? If it is not true for any planet, find out for which planet and why.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Earlier, mercury thermometers were used for measuring the body temperature. But mercury is an extremely toxic substance and is difficult to dispose of if the thermometer breaks accidently.
texts.text: Earlier, mercury thermometers were used for measuring the body temperature. But mercury is an extremely toxic substance and is difficult to dispose of if the thermometer breaks accidently.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Digital thermometers pose no such risk and also the numbers in its display are easier to read. Therefore, mercury thermometers are being replaced by digital thermometers. Temperature in a digital thermometer is determined with the help of heat sensors.
texts.text: Digital thermometers pose no such risk and also the numbers in its display are easier to read. Therefore, mercury thermometers are being replaced by digital thermometers. Temperature in a digital thermometer is determined with the help of heat sensors.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The liquid used in the laboratory thermometer is generally alcohol (coloured red to make it easily seen) or mercury.
texts.text: The liquid used in the laboratory thermometer is generally alcohol (coloured red to make it easily seen) or mercury.

texts.orig: Read the temperatures of ice and boiling water again after some time. Are the temperatures same or have changed? You may have noticed that the temperature of water remains constant while it is boiling. Also, the temperature of ice remains constant while it is melting.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Caution
texts.text: Caution

texts.label: text
texts.orig: There are many techniques for measuring air temperature. Air temperature is an important weather parameter and monitored at weather stations all over the world. The data gathered on air temperature along with various other parameters are used for making weather forecasts.
texts.text: There are many techniques for measuring air temperature. Air temperature is an important weather parameter and monitored at weather stations all over the world. The data gathered on air temperature along with various other parameters are used for making weather forecasts.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: now a scientist
texts.text: now a scientist

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The temperature at the core of the Sun reaches as high as 15 million degrees Celsius. Are there objects in the sky that have even higher temperatures? There is no limit on the highest temperature that can exist. However, as per scientific understanding, there is a limit to the lowest temperature that can be achieved. It is close to -273.15 °C (0 kelvin) and is called absolute zero.
texts.text: The temperature at the core of the Sun reaches as high as 15 million degrees Celsius. Are there objects in the sky that have even higher temperatures? There is no limit on the highest temperature that can exist. However, as per scientific understanding, there is a limit to the lowest temperature that can be achieved. It is close to -273.15 °C (0 kelvin) and is called absolute zero.
